summary: Romanian rental-contract excerpt. Source includes diacritics (ăîșțâ); fixture author falls back to Helvetica + ASCII transliteration when Noto Sans is unavailable on the generating host. The pinned PdfiumPinned-v1 renderer is the bitmap-identity authority — same source bytes must hash to the same R end-to-end.

disputeScenarios.mutation: Re-author the PDF embedding NotoSans-Regular.ttf in place of the Helvetica fallback (set NOTO_TTF_PATH and re-run RenderCommit.Cli generate-fixtures). The glyph shapes change for non-ASCII codepoints and any diacritic-bearing word renders with different pixels.
disputeScenarios.expectedResult: Disputed — committed R covers Helvetica-rendered ASCII text; R' over the Noto-rendered diacritic text diverges.

verifiedScenarios.expectedResult: Verified — bytes unchanged, PdfiumPinned-v1 deterministic on the committed source.

notes: ADR-0008 §Test fixtures attack class 'font-fallback divergence'. Mladenov et al. NDSS 2021 §3.2.
